articles.title: Ancient Octopus Giants: 20-Meter-Long Apex Predators of the Cretaceous
articles.summary: Imagine an octopus as long as a school bus! Fossil jaws reveal that ancient octopuses were giant apex predators that ruled the oceans 100 million years ago, competing with marine reptiles.

articles.title: Adrenaline: The Hormone That Powers Your Fight-or-Flight Response
articles.summary: Ever feel your heart pound before a test or a scary movie? That's adrenaline! Learn how this hormone helps you react fast, why too much can be harmful, and how it's used as lifesaving medicine.

articles.title: Iberian Lynxes Caught Soaking Their Prey: A First for Carnivores
articles.summary: Camera traps in Spain captured wild Iberian lynxes dunking dead rabbits in water—a first-ever behavior among meat-eaters. Scientists think they're softening the meat for cubs or adding hydration, revealing surprising intelligence.
